diagrams.title: Grid Cluster Distributed Computing Architecture
diagrams.description: A high-level architectural overview showing the hierarchical structure of a grid cluster distributed computing system, including client requests, resource management layer, and compute node clusters.
elements: Cluster of compute nodes (Node 1, Node 2, Node 3, Node 4)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how a distributed computing system is organized, showing the flow of computational tasks from users through management infrastructure to compute resources and back.

diagrams.title: Cluster Computing Topology
diagrams.description: A detailed view of a homogeneous cluster computing setup where multiple nodes are tightly coupled through a high-speed network, often used in HPC (High Performance Computing) environments.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the tight coupling and low-latency communication in cluster computing, where nodes work together as a single unified resource under coordinated management.

diagrams.title: Grid Computing Resource Pool
diagrams.description: A conceptual diagram showing geographically distributed heterogeneous resources in a grid computing environment, combining computing power from multiple organizations.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the decentralized, heterogeneous nature of grid computing where resources from different administrative domains are pooled together to create a virtual supercomputer.
